visa_types.overview:
Overview
A visa is only intended to allow a foreign national to visit Belgium for a limited period of time. This category of visa caters to people wishing to visit Belgium for touristic purposes either through buying a tour via travel agency, or through self-bookings of tickets and hotel. Applicants will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category.
Guarantees for return (assessment of migration risk): The applicant is requested to provide as much information as possible to document his/her family background, professional and socio-economic status, ownership of land or real estate (for instance : proof of family bonds with his/her country of origin, proof of regular income and stable source of revenues from the applicant and/or his/her partner, proof of property, etc.)
This information is essential to correctly judge the intention of the applicant to leave the Schengen area before the expiry of his/her visa.
Please click on each of the above tab for detailed information. 
Note: In case you have already submitted your fingerprints for a previous Schengen visa application in the last 59 months, you are not required to submit them again.
However, If you are uncertain whether your fingerprints have been captured for a Schengen visa within the last 59 months, you are advised to appear in person.
Kindly note that, if it would appear that the fingerprints prove not to be present in the VIS system, you will be asked to reappear in person for new biometric data collection. To avoid this possible complication, you have the right to ask for your fingerprints to be taken again.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
A visa is only intended to allow a foreign national to visit Belgium for a limited period of time. This category of visa caters to people wishing to visit Belgium for touristic purposes either through buying a tour via travel agency, or through self-bookings of tickets and hotel. Applicants will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category.
Guarantees for return (assessment of migration risk): The applicant is requested to provide as much information as possible to document his/her family background, professional and socio-economic status, ownership of land or real estate (for instance : proof of family bonds with his/her country of origin, proof of regular income and stable source of revenues from the applicant and/or his/her partner, proof of property, etc.)
This information is essential to correctly judge the intention of the applicant to leave the Schengen area before the expiry of his/her visa.
Please click on each of the above tab for detailed information. 
Note: In case you have already submitted your fingerprints for a previous Schengen visa application in the last 59 months, you are not required to submit them again.
However, If you are uncertain whether your fingerprints have been captured for a Schengen visa within the last 59 months, you are advised to appear in person.
Kindly note that, if it would appear that the fingerprints prove not to be present in the VIS system, you will be asked to reappear in person for new biometric data collection. To avoid this possible complication, you have the right to ask for your fingerprints to be taken again.

## Visa Fees

[bel > tur > en > Short Term Visa > Table: Visa Fees 1]
Please Note: 
- There is a service charge of 1164 TL (24 EUR) (Inclusive of 20% VAT) per application over and above the visa fees for applicants who apply from Visa Application Centers.
                                                                            
- The visa application centre offers an optional service at a nominal additional cost:

[bel > tur > en > Table: Vas Fees]
- Please click here for more information on the optional  service (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/tur/en/bel/premium-services).
- The mandatory payments (VFS service Fee) and optional services (Courier delivery, Photocopy and Photobooth service) are to be paid in cash only in Turkish Lira.
Note:
All fees and service charge listed above are non-refundable and may only be paid in cash, please note change will be given in Turkish Lira.

## Documents Required
Seafarers (Visa C)

## Photo Specifications
A Photograph submitted    with the application for a visa should comply with the following    specifications:
- Must be an up-to-date photograph (dated max 6 month old)
- Must be a color photograph printed on normal photographic paper
- Should be sized between 35 – 45 mm
- Full face taken straight forward 30 - 36 mm from point of the chin to top of hair
- Taken against a light background
- Without sunglasses
- Without hat or other head covering unless you wear this for religious reasons
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses
- The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes
- The mouth must be shut
- Applicants should clearly write their name and surname on the back of their photographs
For further details with samples click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/t9TtaOllA1GFmugnzwyL5/2738c0ad38342f13eaea02c8d65e89a5/photo.pdf) 
Please note that the Visa application Centre may not accept photographs that do not meet these specifications. In case photos are not meeting the requirements mentioned above Submission Officer might ask to provide updated / recent photos.

## Processing Time
Since our work is primarily administrative in nature, we have no say on whether you will be granted a visa and how long it will take to process, as this is entirely the prerogative of the Consulate General of Belgium.
To follow-up a visa application, please find information on Follow up a visa application - IBZ (https://dofi.ibz.be/en/themes/faq/visa/follow-visa-application)
Procesing time for visa applications :
Short stay (C visa): Schengen area entry visa application (C visa) (https://dofi.ibz.be/en/themes/ressortissants-dun-pays-tiers/court-sejour/schengen-area-entry-visa-application-visa-c) (See Processing time for a visa application)
Family reunification (D visa): Visa application (https://dofi.ibz.be/en/themes/third-country-nationals/family-reunification/visa-d-application-family-reunification) (See Processing time for a visa application)
Studies (D visa): first residence permit (application for a D visa, a residence permit or a change of status) (https://dofi.ibz.be/en/themes/third-country-nationals/study/initial-authorisation-stay-application-visa-d-authorisation) (See Processing time for a visa application)
Application for a residence permit (D visa): the law does not set a time limit within which the Immigration Office has to make a decision. As the number of applications is currently very high, the processing time is usually long (several months).
Single permit: Single permit (https://dofi.ibz.be/en/themas/onderdanen-van-derde-landen/werk/single-permit) (See Processing time for an application)
Application for a visa for marriage or cohabitation in Belgium (D visa):the law does not set a time limit within which the Immigration Office has to make a decision. This application is generally processed within an average of 3.5 months to 4 months from the date of submission of the visa application. 
Application for an entry visa for a family member of a citizen of the European Union or an associated country (C visa): see Short stay

## Download Forms
Please click here to fill online visa application form (https://visaonweb.diplomatie.be/)
Please note that personal appearance may be required; a Visa / Immigration officer may require a personal interview at The Consulate General of Belgium, In Istanbul prior to a decision being taken on your application.

visa_types.documents_required:
Passport + copy
A valid passport:
- should have been issued within the previous 10 years;
- should be valid at least 3 months after the intended date of departure from the Schengen territory or, in the case of several visits, after the last intended date of departure; - should contain at least 2 empty pages when applying for the visa.
Provide one copy of the passport (the empty pages don’t need to be copied) with Schengen and others visas + exit /entry stamp of the previous passport.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
From 1/12/2022, Turkish passengers who have a connection at an airport in Belgium before boarding to another country, will have in their passport a new visa that only allows them to pass through the international transit area of a Belgian airport.
This is the airport transit visa (A visa). 
This visa allows only a transit in the international zone or in the zone under the authority of the customs at the airport. It does not allow the passenger to leave the airport.
All A visas will be requested from our partner VFS Global on the basis of an appointment. Applicants are advised to submit their complete visa files at least 15 working days before the intended date of departure.
Several categories of travelers are exempt from A visa, see: 
French version :  2.1 Annexe Exception pour le visa de transit aéroportuaire visa Visa type A.pdf (ibz.be) (https://protect-eu.mimecast.com/s/I0V5CkovoUZqLlKs2_f6g?domain=eur01.safelinks.protection.outlook.com)
Dutch version : 2.1 Bijlage Uitzonderingen op de transitvisumplicht voor luchthavens Visum type A visum.pdf (ibz.be) (https://protect-eu.mimecast.com/s/udQEClpwphDz9GVh9_sAH?domain=dofi.ibz.be)
NOTE
Our Consulate may in justified individual cases, request additional documents during the examination of an application which are not mentioned in the above harmonized list. The applicant is hereby informed that submitting the above-mentioned documents does not guarantee automatic issuance of a visa.
Non-complete files will be treated as such and sent to the Ministry of the Interior / Immigration Office in Brussels. Incomplete files may lead to a refusal of the visa.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
A visa is only intended to allow a foreign national to visit Belgium for a limited period of time. This category of visa caters for people wishing visit the member of his/her family or friend. 
Applicant will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category.
Guarantees for return (assessment of migration risk): The applicant is requested to provide as much information as possible to document his/her family background, professional and socio-economic status, ownership of land or real estate (for instance : proof of family bonds with his/her country of origin, proof of regular income and stable source of revenues from the applicant and/or his/her partner, proof of property, etc.)
This information is essential to correctly judge the intention of the applicant to leave the Schengen area before the expiry of his/her visa.
Please click on each of the above tab for detailed information.
Note: In case you have already submitted your fingerprints for a previous Schengen visa application in the last 59 months, you are not required to submit them again.
However, If you are uncertain whether your fingerprints have been captured for a Schengen visa within the last 59 months, you are advised to appear in person.
Kindly note that, if it would appear that the fingerprints prove not to be present in the VIS system, you will be asked to reappear in person for new biometric data collection. To avoid this possible complication, you have the right to ask for your fingerprints to be taken again.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
A visa is only intended to allow a foreign national to visit Belgium for a limited period of time. This category of visa caters to people wishing to visit Belgium for touristic purposes either through buying a tour via travel agency, or through self-bookings of tickets and hotel. Applicants will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category.
Guarantees for return (assessment of migration risk): The applicant is requested to provide as much information as possible to document his/her family background, professional and socio-economic status, ownership of land or real estate (for instance : proof of family bonds with his/her country of origin, proof of regular income and stable source of revenues from the applicant and/or his/her partner, proof of property, etc.)
This information is essential to correctly judge the intention of the applicant to leave the Schengen area before the expiry of his/her visa.
Please click on each of the above tab for detailed information. 
Note: In case you have already submitted your fingerprints for a previous Schengen visa application in the last 59 months, you are not required to submit them again.
However, If you are uncertain whether your fingerprints have been captured for a Schengen visa within the last 59 months, you are advised to appear in person.
Kindly note that, if it would appear that the fingerprints prove not to be present in the VIS system, you will be asked to reappear in person for new biometric data collection. To avoid this possible complication, you have the right to ask for your fingerprints to be taken again.

A Photograph submitted    with the application for a visa should comply with the following    specifications:

- Full face taken straight forward 30 - 36 mm from point of the chin to top of hair
- Taken against a light background
- Without sunglasses
- Without hat or other head covering unless you wear this for religious reasons
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses
- The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes
- The mouth must be shut
- Applicants should clearly write their name and surname on the back of their photographs

visa_types.overview:
Overview
A visa is only intended to allow a foreign national to visit Belgium for a limited period of time. This category of visa caters to people wishing to visit Belgium for touristic purposes either through buying a tour via travel agency, or through self-bookings of tickets and hotel. 
Applicants will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category.
Guarantees for return (assessment of migration risk): The applicant is requested to provide as much information as possible to document his/her family background, professional and socio-economic status, ownership of land or real estate (for instance : proof of family bonds with his/her country of origin, proof of regular income and stable source of revenues from the applicant and/or his/her partner, proof of property, etc.)
This information is essential to correctly judge the intention of the applicant to leave the Schengen area before the expiry of his/her visa.
Please click on each of the above tab for detailed information. 
Note: In case you have already submitted your fingerprints for a previous Schengen visa application in the last 59 months, you are not required to submit them again.
However, If you are uncertain whether your fingerprints have been captured for a Schengen visa within the last 59 months, you are advised to appear in person.
Kindly note that, if it would appear that the fingerprints prove not to be present in the VIS system, you will be asked to reappear in person for new biometric data collection. To avoid this possible complication, you have the right to ask for your fingerprints to be taken again.

## Overview
 A visa is only intended to allow a foreign national to visit Belgium for a limited period of time. This category of visa caters for people wishing to visit Belgium with purpose of participation in Cultural or Sport event.  Applicant will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category.
Guarantees for return (assessment of migration risk): The applicant is requested to provide as much information as possible to document his/her family background, professional and socio-economic status, ownership of land or real estate (for instance : proof of family bonds with his/her country of origin, proof of regular income and stable source of revenues from the applicant and/or his/her partner, proof of property, etc.)
This information is essential to correctly judge the intention of the applicant to leave the Schengen area before the expiry of his/her visa.
Please click on each of the above tab for detailed information. 
Note: In case you have already submitted your fingerprints for a previous Schengen visa application in the last 59 months, you are not required to submit them again.
However, If you are uncertain whether your fingerprints have been captured for a Schengen visa within the last 59 months, you are advised to appear in person.
Kindly note that, if it would appear that the fingerprints prove not to be present in the VIS system, you will be asked to reappear in person for new biometric data collection. To avoid this possible complication, you have the right to ask for your fingerprints to be taken again.

## Visa Fees

[bel > tur > en > Short Term Visa > Table: Visa Fees 1]
Please Note: 
- There is a service charge of 1164 TL (24 EUR) (Inclusive of 20% VAT) per application over and above the visa fees for applicants who apply from Visa Application Centers.
                                                                            
- The visa application centre offers an optional service at a nominal additional cost:

[bel > tur > en > Table: Vas Fees]
- Please click here for more information on the optional  service (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/tur/en/bel/premium-services).
- The mandatory payments (VFS service Fee) and optional services (Courier delivery, Photocopy and Photobooth service) are to be paid in cash only in Turkish Lira.
Note:
All fees and service charge listed above are non-refundable and may only be paid in cash, please note change will be given in Turkish Lira.

## Documents Required
Academic Cultural Conference Sport Event (Visa C)

## Photo Specifications
A Photograph submitted    with the application for a visa should comply with the following    specifications:
- Must be an up-to-date photograph (dated max 6 month old)
- Must be a color photograph printed on normal photographic paper
- Should be sized between 35 – 45 mm
- Full face taken straight forward 30 - 36 mm from point of the chin to top of hair
- Taken against a light background
- Without sunglasses
- Without hat or other head covering unless you wear this for religious reasons
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses
- The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes
- The mouth must be shut
- Applicants should clearly write their name and surname on the back of their photographs
For further details with samples click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/t9TtaOllA1GFmugnzwyL5/2738c0ad38342f13eaea02c8d65e89a5/photo.pdf) 
Please note that the Visa application Centre may not accept photographs that do not meet these specifications. In case photos are not meeting the requirements mentioned above Submission Officer might ask to provide updated / recent photos.

## Processing Time
Since our work is primarily administrative in nature, we have no say on whether you will be granted a visa and how long it will take to process, as this is entirely the prerogative of the Consulate General of Belgium.
To follow-up a visa application, please find information on Follow up a visa application - IBZ (https://dofi.ibz.be/en/themes/faq/visa/follow-visa-application)
Procesing time for visa applications :
Short stay (C visa): Schengen area entry visa application (C visa) (https://dofi.ibz.be/en/themes/ressortissants-dun-pays-tiers/court-sejour/schengen-area-entry-visa-application-visa-c) (See Processing time for a visa application)
Family reunification (D visa): Visa application (https://dofi.ibz.be/en/themes/third-country-nationals/family-reunification/visa-d-application-family-reunification) (See Processing time for a visa application)
Studies (D visa): first residence permit (application for a D visa, a residence permit or a change of status) (https://dofi.ibz.be/en/themes/third-country-nationals/study/initial-authorisation-stay-application-visa-d-authorisation) (See Processing time for a visa application)
Application for a residence permit (D visa): the law does not set a time limit within which the Immigration Office has to make a decision. As the number of applications is currently very high, the processing time is usually long (several months).
Single permit: Single permit (https://dofi.ibz.be/en/themas/onderdanen-van-derde-landen/werk/single-permit) (See Processing time for an application)
Application for a visa for marriage or cohabitation in Belgium (D visa):the law does not set a time limit within which the Immigration Office has to make a decision. This application is generally processed within an average of 3.5 months to 4 months from the date of submission of the visa application. 
Application for an entry visa for a family member of a citizen of the European Union or an associated country (C visa): see Short stay

## Download Forms
Please click here to fill online visa application form (https://visaonweb.diplomatie.be/)
Please note that personal appearance may be required; a Visa / Immigration officer may require a personal interview at The Consulate General of Belgium, In Istanbul prior to a decision being taken on your application.
